SEARCH
Module it.polimi.ingsw.am37
Package it.polimi.ingsw.am37.model.player

Class Player

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am37.model.player.Player
public class Player extends Object
The Player class contains all the information and behaviour of a user of the application.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Player(String nickname)
    The Player(nickname, token) constructor sets the nickname and the token of the player to the ones given as parameters.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    drawCardFromAvailable(StandardCard card)
    The drawCardFromAvailable(card) method adds the available Card chosen by the player to his hand.
    void
    drawCardFromDeck(GoldDeck deck)
    The drawCardFromDeck(deck) method adds a Gold Card to the hand of the player by drawing it from the deck given as a parameter.
    void
    drawCardFromDeck(ResourceDeck deck)
    The drawCardFromDeck(deck) method adds a Resource Card to the hand of the player by drawing it from the deck given as a parameter.
    GameModel
    getGame()
    The getGame() method returns the game where the player is currently playing.
    List<StandardCard>
    getHand()
    The getHand() method returns the current hand of the player, which is a list of three GameCards.
    Kingdom
    getMyKingdom()
    The getMyKingdom() method returns a reference to the kingdom of the player.
    String
    getNickname()
    The getNickname() method returns the nickname of the player, which is a string.
    ObjectiveCard[]
    getObjectivesToChooseFrom()
    the method getObjectivesToChooseFrom returns the objectives that the player will have to choose from.
    ObjectiveCard
    getPrivateObjective()
    The getPrivateObjective() method returns the private objective of the player and will be called during the points' final calculation after the endgame phase.
    StartCard
    getStartCard()
    The getStartCard() method returns the startCard assigned to the player at the beginning of the game.
    Token
    getToken()
    The getToken() method returns the token related to the player.
    void
    instantiateMyKingdom(StartCard sC, Side startSide)
    The instantiateMyKingdom(sC, startSide) method creates a new Kingdom for the Player with the StartCard and Side that are given as parameters.
    void
    placeCard(StandardCard card, Side toPlace, Position position)
    The placeCard(placed, position) method checks if the Side given as a parameter is the Side of one of the Card in the Player's hand, then it checks if the Position given as a parameter is part of the list of currently active positions in the Kingdom.
    void
    setGame(GameModel game)
    The setGame(game) method checks if the player already has a linked game and if it hasn't, it links the player to the game he is currently playing, which is given as a parameter.
    void
    setHand(List<StandardCard> hand)
    The setHand(hand) method checks if the hand of the player has been initialized and if it hasn't it assigns the hand attribute to the list of cards given as parameters.
    void
    setObjectivesToChooseFrom(ObjectiveCard[] objectivesToChooseFrom)
    the method setObjectivesToChooseFrom sets the objectives from which the player will have to choose one.
    void
    setPrivateObjective(ObjectiveCard c)
    The setPrivateObjective(c) method sets the Player's private ObjectiveCard to the one given as a parameter.
    void
    setStartCard(StartCard startCard)
    The setStartCard(startCard) method checks if the startCard has already been assigned to the player and if it hasn't it assigns the startCard attribute to the card given as a parameter.
    void
    setToken(Token token)
    The setToken(token)method checks if the player already has a linked token and if it hasn't, it links the player to the token he has chosen, which is given as a parameter.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Player

      public Player(String nickname)
      The Player(nickname, token) constructor sets the nickname and the token of the player to the ones given as parameters.
      Parameters:
      nickname - A string that uniquely identifies the player.

  • Method Details

    • getNickname

      public String getNickname()
      The getNickname() method returns the nickname of the player, which is a string.
      Returns:
      The nickname attribute.

    • getToken

      public Token getToken()
      The getToken() method returns the token related to the player.
      Returns:
      The token attribute.

    • setToken

      public void setToken(Token token) throws AlreadyAssignedException
      The setToken(token)method checks if the player already has a linked token and if it hasn't, it links the player to the token he has chosen, which is given as a parameter. It is only called once, at the beginning of the game. If it is called a second time, the method will throw an AlreadyAssignedException.
      Parameters:
      token - A Token object.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - This attribute cannot be assigned twice.

    • setGame

      public void setGame(GameModel game) throws AlreadyAssignedException
      The setGame(game) method checks if the player already has a linked game and if it hasn't, it links the player to the game he is currently playing, which is given as a parameter. It is only called once, at the beginning of the game. If it is called a second time, the method will throw an AlreadyAssignedException.
      Parameters:
      game - A GameModel object where the player is part of the list of the participants.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - This attribute cannot be assigned twice.

    • getGame

      public GameModel getGame()
      The getGame() method returns the game where the player is currently playing.
      Returns:
      The game attribute.

    • setHand

      public void setHand(List<StandardCard> hand) throws AlreadyAssignedException
      The setHand(hand) method checks if the hand of the player has been initialized and if it hasn't it assigns the hand attribute to the list of cards given as parameters. It is only called once, at the beginning of the game. If it is called a second time, the method will throw an AlreadyAssignedException.
      Parameters:
      hand - A list of card that will become the initial hand of the player.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - This attribute cannot be assigned twice.

    • getHand

      public List<StandardCard> getHand()
      The getHand() method returns the current hand of the player, which is a list of three GameCards.
      Returns:
      The hand attribute.

    • setStartCard

      public void setStartCard(StartCard startCard) throws AlreadyAssignedException
      The setStartCard(startCard) method checks if the startCard has already been assigned to the player and if it hasn't it assigns the startCard attribute to the card given as a parameter. It is only called once, at the beginning of the game. If it is called a second time, the method will throw an AlreadyAssignedException.
      Parameters:
      startCard - A card of type startCard.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - This attribute cannot be assigned twice.

    • getStartCard

      public StartCard getStartCard()
      The getStartCard() method returns the startCard assigned to the player at the beginning of the game.
      Returns:
      The startCard attribute.

    • getObjectivesToChooseFrom

      public ObjectiveCard[] getObjectivesToChooseFrom()
      the method getObjectivesToChooseFrom returns the objectives that the player will have to choose from.
      Returns:
      the objectivesToChooseFrom attribute.

    • setObjectivesToChooseFrom

      public void setObjectivesToChooseFrom(ObjectiveCard[] objectivesToChooseFrom) throws AlreadyAssignedException
      the method setObjectivesToChooseFrom sets the objectives from which the player will have to choose one.
      Parameters:
      objectivesToChooseFrom - are the objectives assigned to the player.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the objectives have already been assigned to the player.

    • setPrivateObjective

      public void setPrivateObjective(ObjectiveCard c) throws AlreadyAssignedException
      The setPrivateObjective(c) method sets the Player's private ObjectiveCard to the one given as a parameter.
      Parameters:
      c - The ObjectiveCard that the player has chosen.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - Throws this exception if the private Objective has already been assigned.

    • getPrivateObjective

      public ObjectiveCard getPrivateObjective()
      The getPrivateObjective() method returns the private objective of the player and will be called during the points' final calculation after the endgame phase.
      Returns:
      The privateObjective attribute.

    • instantiateMyKingdom

      public void instantiateMyKingdom(StartCard sC, Side startSide) throws AlreadyAssignedException, IncorrectUserActionException
      The instantiateMyKingdom(sC, startSide) method creates a new Kingdom for the Player with the StartCard and Side that are given as parameters.
      Parameters:
      sC - The StartCard that the Player drew.
      startSide - The StartCard Side that the Player chose.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - Throws this exception if the Kingdom has already been created.
      IncorrectUserActionException - Throws this exception if the Kingdom cannot be created.

    • getMyKingdom

      public Kingdom getMyKingdom()
      The getMyKingdom() method returns a reference to the kingdom of the player.
      Returns:
      The myKingdom attribute.

    • drawCardFromDeck

      public void drawCardFromDeck(ResourceDeck deck) throws NoCardsException, IncorrectUserActionException
      The drawCardFromDeck(deck) method adds a Resource Card to the hand of the player by drawing it from the deck given as a parameter.
      Parameters:
      deck - The deck from which the Player has chosen to draw the Card.
      Throws:
      NoCardsException - when the deck is empty.
      IncorrectUserActionException - when the player already has 3 cards in his hands.

    • drawCardFromDeck

      public void drawCardFromDeck(GoldDeck deck) throws NoCardsException, IncorrectUserActionException
      The drawCardFromDeck(deck) method adds a Gold Card to the hand of the player by drawing it from the deck given as a parameter.
      Parameters:
      deck - The deck from which the Player has chosen to draw the Card.
      Throws:
      NoCardsException - when the deck is empty.
      IncorrectUserActionException - when the player already has 3 cards in his hands.

    • drawCardFromAvailable

      public void drawCardFromAvailable(StandardCard card) throws NoCardsException, IncorrectUserActionException
      The drawCardFromAvailable(card) method adds the available Card chosen by the player to his hand. Then it removes the available Card from the list of available Cards and draws a new Card from the corresponding deck and adds it to the available Cards.
      Parameters:
      card - The card that the Player wants to draw.
      Throws:
      NoCardsException - when the deck is empty.
      IncorrectUserActionException - when the player already has 3 cards in his hands or the card is not available.

    • placeCard

      public void placeCard(StandardCard card, Side toPlace, Position position) throws IncorrectUserActionException
      The placeCard(placed, position) method checks if the Side given as a parameter is the Side of one of the Card in the Player's hand, then it checks if the Position given as a parameter is part of the list of currently active positions in the Kingdom. Finally, it checks for any given placementCondition of the Card (only if the Side placed is a Front) and if it is fulfilled it places the Card and updates the Kingdom by calling the updateKingdom() method. If any of these check fails it doesn't place the Card and lets the player know that the command failed.
      Parameters:
      card - The Card that the Player wants to place from his hand.
      toPlace - The Side of the Card that the Player wants to place.
      position - The Position where the Player wants to place the Side.
      Throws:
      IncorrectUserActionException - if the card is not in the hand, the position is wrong, the resource condition is not satisfied or the side is not coherent with the card.
